sentences.en: I wake up at seven in the morning.
I wash my face and brush my teeth.
And then I eat breakfast.
Today I eat toast and eggs.
I drink coffee.
I get dressed and pack my bag.
I take the bus and go to work.
I work at the office.

quiz.q:
저는 몇 시에 일어나요?
(What time do I wake up?)
opts: 여섯 시에 일어나요 (wake up at six)
일곱 시에 일어나요 (wake up at seven)
여덟 시에 일어나요 (wake up at eight)
아홉 시에 일어나요 (wake up at nine)

quiz.q:
아침에 무엇을 해요?
(What do I do in the morning?)
opts: 세수를 하고 이를 닦아요 (wash face and brush teeth)

quiz.q:
오늘 아침에 무엇을 먹어요?
(What do I eat for breakfast today?)

저는 무엇을 마셔요?
(What do I drink?)

저는 어디에 가요?
(Where do I go?)

sentences.en: Minsu meets a friend on Saturday.
The two people go to a small cafe.
Minsu orders an iced Americano.
His friend drinks warm tea.
The cafe is quiet and has a nice atmosphere.
The two people talk a lot.
They take pictures and eat cake.
They had a great time.

quiz.q:
민수는 언제 친구를 만나요?
(When does Minsu meet his friend?)

두 사람은 어디에 가요?
(Where do the two people go?)

민수는 무엇을 주문해요?
(What does Minsu order?)

카페는 어때요?
(How is the cafe?)
opts: 조용하고 분위기가 좋아요 (quiet with a nice atmosphere)
넓고 사람이 없어요 (wide with no people)

quiz.q:
두 사람은 무엇을 먹어요?
(What do the two people eat?)

enTitle: Preparing for a Trip to Korea

sentences.kr: 저는 다음 달에 한국에 가려고 해요.
sentences.en: I am planning to go to Korea next month.
I want to meet my friend in Korea.
So I slowly make a travel plan.
First, I check the flight ticket.
And I look up the hotel location.
I plan to take the subway a lot in Seoul.
I prepare a map so I do not get lost.
I want to order in Korean and ask for directions too.
I am a little nervous, but I am really excited.

quiz.q:
저는 언제 한국에 가려고 해요?
(When am I planning to go to Korea?)
opts: 오늘 가려고 해요 (planning to go today)
내일 가려고 해요 (planning to go tomorrow)
다음 달에 가려고 해요 (planning to go next month)
내년에 가려고 해요 (planning to go next year)

quiz.q:
저는 한국에서 누구를 만나고 싶어요?
(Who do I want to meet in Korea?)

먼저 무엇을 확인해요?
(What do I check first?)
opts: 비행기 표를 확인해요 (check the flight ticket)
음식 메뉴를 확인해요 (check the food menu)

quiz.q:
서울에서 무엇을 많이 타려고 해요?
(What do I plan to take a lot in Seoul?)

왜 지도를 준비해요?
(Why do I prepare a map?)
opts: 길을 잃지 않으려고 준비해요 (so I don't get lost)
친구를 찾으려고 준비해요 (to find a friend)
호텔을 예약하려고 준비해요 (to book a hotel)

sentences.en: My family has four people.

sentences.kr: 아버지와 어머니, 그리고 남동생이 한 명 있어요.
sentences.en: I have a father, a mother, and one younger brother.
My father goes to work.
My mother is an elementary school teacher.
My younger brother is a high school student.
I am a university student.
My family gets along well.

quiz.q:
제 가족은 몇 명이에요?
(How many people are in my family?)

누가 회사에 가요?
(Who goes to work?)

어머니는 뭐예요?
(What is my mother?)

남동생은 뭐예요?
(What is my younger brother?)

우리 가족은 어때요?
(How is my family?)

sentences.en: I go to school at 8 in the morning.
I learn Korean at school.
The teacher is kind.
I talk a lot with friends at school.
At lunch, I eat with friends.
And then I go to the library.
I read books and study at the library.
School life is fun.

quiz.q:
저는 몇 시에 학교에 가요?
(What time do I go to school?)

학교에서 무엇을 배워요?
(What do I learn at school?)

선생님은 어때요?
(How is the teacher?)

점심에 누구하고 밥을 먹어요?
(Who do I eat lunch with?)

도서관에서 무엇을 해요?
(What do I do at the library?)

sentences.en: There are many convenience stores in Korea.
Convenience stores sell many things.
There are daily necessities, and there is also a lot of food and drinks.
People buy a lot of triangle gimbap and cup ramen.
They pay and eat at the convenience store.
Convenience stores are really convenient.

quiz.q:
한국에는 무엇이 많아요?
(What are there many of in Korea?)

편의점에서 무엇을 많이 팔아요?
(What do convenience stores sell a lot of?)

편의점에는 음식하고 무엇이 많아요?
(There is a lot of food and what else?)

사람들은 무엇을 많이 사요?
(What do people buy a lot?)
opts: 삼각김밥하고 컵라면 (triangle gimbap and cup ramen)

quiz.q:
편의점은 어때요?
(How are convenience stores?)

sentences.en: Korea has four seasons.
Spring is warm and many flowers bloom.
Summer is hot and it rains a lot.
People go to the beach.

sentences.kr: 가을은 선선해서 사람들이 야외활동을 많이 해요.
sentences.en: Fall is cool and people go out a lot for outdoor activities .
The fall leaves are very beautiful too.
Winter is cold and it snows.
Fall is my favorite season.

quiz.q:
한국에는 무엇이 있어요?
(What does Korea have?)

봄에는 무엇이 많이 펴요?
(What blooms a lot in spring?)

여름 날씨는 어때요?
(How is summer?)

가을에는 무엇이 예뻐요?
(What is beautiful in autumn?)

제가 가장 좋아하는 계절은 무엇이에요?
(What is my favorite season?)

sentences.kr: 우리 집에는 강아지 한 마리가 있어요.
sentences.en: There is one dog in my house.
Its name is Mungchi.
Mungchi is four years old this year.
It is big, but very cute.
It likes playing with bubbles.
It goes close to strangers.
But it dislikes baths.
So it is difficult to give it a bath.

quiz.q:
강아지 이름은 무엇이에요?
(What is the dog's name?)

뭉치는 몇 살이에요?
(How old is Mungchi?)

뭉치는 무엇을 좋아해요?
(What does Mungchi like?)

뭉치는 낯선 사람을 보면 어떻게 해요?
(What does Mungchi do when seeing strangers?)

뭉치는 무엇을 싫어해요?
(What does Mungchi dislike?)

sentences.en: It is raining today.
I go out with an umbrella.
When it rains, the weather is cool.
I go to a cafe and drink coffee.
I relax while looking outside.
My friend does not like rain.
So my friend does not like summer either.
I like rainy days.

quiz.q:
오늘 날씨는 어때요?
(How is the weather today?)

저는 무엇을 가지고 나가요?
(What do I take with me?)

저는 어디에서 커피를 마셔요?
(Where do I drink coffee?)

친구는 무엇을 안 좋아해요?
(What does my friend dislike?)

저는 비 오는 날을 어떻게 생각해요?
(How do I feel about rainy days?)

sentences.en: Hello.
My name is Kim Suji.
I live in Incheon.
I am seventeen years old.
I like drawing comics.
I want to become a webtoon artist in the future.
I am looking for a Korean friend.
It would be nice to talk together.
I will wait for your reply.

quiz.q:
수지는 어디에 살고 있어요?
(Where does Suji live?)

수지는 몇 살이에요?
(How old is Suji?)

수지는 무엇을 좋아해요?
(What does Suji like?)

수지는 나중에 무엇이 되고 싶어요?
(What does Suji want to become?)

수지는 지금 무엇을 찾고 있어요?
(What is Suji looking for?)

sentences.en: I came to Thailand for a trip.
I am in Bangkok now.
The weather is warm, so it is nice for traveling.
The people are also very kind.
One time, I got lost.
But a woman helped me.

sentences.kr: 저는 여행하면서 친구를 사귀는 것을 좋아해요.
sentences.en: I like making friends while traveling.
Meeting new people is fun.

quiz.q:
지금 어디에 있어요?
(Where am I now?)

방콕 날씨는 어때요?
(How is the weather in Bangkok?)

한 번은 무슨 일이 있었어요?
(What happened one time?)

누가 도와줬어요?
(Who helped me?)

저는 여행하면서 무엇을 좋아해요?
(What do I like while traveling?)

sentences.en: It has been two years since I started studying Korean.

sentences.kr: 처음에는 단어를 많이 외우는 것이 가장 중요하다고 생각했어요.
sentences.en: At first, I thought memorizing many words was the most important thing.

sentences.kr: 하지만 시간이 지나면서 듣기와 읽기도 중요하다는 것을 알게 되었어요.
sentences.en: But as time passed, I realized that listening and reading are also important.

sentences.kr: 그래서 매일 한국 드라마를 보고 짧은 글도 읽고 있어요.
sentences.en: So I watch Korean dramas and read short texts every day.

sentences.kr: 모르는 단어가 나오면 바로 사전을 찾기보다 문맥으로 이해하려고 해요.
sentences.en: When I see an unknown word, I try to understand it from context rather than looking it up right away.
Of course, there are still many difficult expressions.

sentences.kr: 그래도 예전보다 한국어가 훨씬 자연스럽게 들려요.
sentences.en: Still, Korean sounds much more natural to me than before.

sentences.kr: 앞으로는 더 다양한 책을 읽어 보고 싶어요.
sentences.en: In the future, I want to try reading more various books.

quiz.q:
저는 한국어를 공부한 지 얼마나 되었어요?
(How long have I studied Korean?)

처음에는 무엇이 가장 중요하다고 생각했어요?
(What did I think was most important at first?)

시간이 지나면서 무엇도 중요하다는 것을 알게 되었어요?
(What did I realize was also important?)

모르는 단어가 나오면 어떻게 하려고 해요?
(What do I try to do with unknown words?)

앞으로 무엇을 읽어 보고 싶어요?
(What do I want to read in the future?)

sentences.kr: 예전에는 혼자 여행하는 것이 무섭다고 생각했어요.
sentences.en: In the past, I thought traveling alone was scary.

sentences.kr: 하지만 처음으로 혼자 여행을 다녀온 후 생각이 바뀌었어요.
sentences.en: But after traveling alone for the first time, my thoughts changed.

sentences.kr: 혼자 여행하면 모든 일정을 스스로 정해야 해요.
sentences.en: When you travel alone, you have to decide the whole schedule yourself.

sentences.kr: 그래서 책임감도 생기고 새로운 경험도 많이 할 수 있어요.
sentences.en: So you gain responsibility and can have many new experiences.

sentences.kr: 물론 가끔 외롭다고 느낄 때도 있어요.
sentences.en: Of course, sometimes you may feel lonely.

sentences.kr: 그럴 때는 현지 사람들과 이야기하거나 새로운 장소를 찾아가요.
sentences.en: At those times, I talk with local people or visit new places.

sentences.kr: 혼자 여행은 불편한 점도 있지만 배울 점도 많아요.
sentences.en: Traveling alone has inconvenient parts, but there is also a lot to learn.

sentences.kr: 그래서 기회가 있으면 다시 혼자 여행해 보고 싶어요.
sentences.en: So if I have the chance, I want to travel alone again.

quiz.q:
예전에는 혼자 여행을 어떻게 생각했어요?
(How did I think about traveling alone before?)

언제 생각이 바뀌었어요?
(When did my thoughts change?)

혼자 여행하면 무엇을 스스로 정해야 해요?
(What do you have to decide yourself?)

가끔 어떻게 느낄 때도 있어요?
(How can one sometimes feel?)

혼자 여행은 불편한 점도 있지만 무엇도 많아요?
(Traveling alone has inconvenient points, but also many what?)

sentences.en: These days, many people communicate through messages.

sentences.kr: 그래서 전화를 하는 경우가 예전보다 줄어들었어요.
sentences.en: So phone calls have decreased compared to before.

sentences.kr: 메시지는 편리하지만 감정을 전달하기 어려울 때가 있어요.
sentences.en: Messages are convenient, but sometimes it is hard to express feelings.

sentences.kr: 반면에 전화는 목소리를 들을 수 있어서 더 자연스럽게 대화할 수 있어요.
sentences.en: On the other hand, phone calls allow you to hear someone's voice, so you can talk more naturally.

sentences.kr: 저는 중요한 이야기를 할 때는 전화하는 편이에요.
sentences.en: When I talk about something important, I tend to call.

sentences.kr: 상황에 따라 적절한 방법을 선택하는 것이 중요하다고 생각해요.
sentences.en: I think it is important to choose the right method depending on the situation.

quiz.q:
요즘은 무엇으로 대화하는 사람이 많아요?
(What do many people use to communicate these days?)

전화는 예전보다 어떻게 되었어요?
(What happened to phone calls compared to before?)

메시지는 무엇을 전달하기 어려울 때가 있어요?
(What can be hard to express through messages?)

전화의 장점은 무엇이에요?
(What is an advantage of phone calls?)

저는 중요한 이야기를 할 때 무엇을 하는 편이에요?
(What do I tend to do for important conversations?)

sentences.kr: 저는 한국 드라마를 보면서 한국어를 공부해요.
sentences.en: I study Korean while watching Korean dramas.

sentences.kr: 처음에는 자막이 없으면 거의 이해하지 못했어요.
sentences.en: At first, I could hardly understand anything without subtitles.

sentences.kr: 하지만 같은 장면을 여러 번 보면서 표현을 익히게 되었어요.
sentences.en: But by watching the same scenes many times, I became familiar with many expressions.

sentences.kr: 특히 일상생활에서 자주 사용하는 표현을 많이 배울 수 있었어요.
sentences.en: I was able to learn many expressions used in daily life.

sentences.kr: 모르는 단어가 나와도 바로 찾지 않고 끝까지 보려고 해요.
sentences.en: Even when I encounter unknown words, I try to keep watching instead of looking them up immediately.

sentences.kr: 그러면 문맥 속에서 의미를 추측하는 연습이 돼요.
sentences.en: That becomes practice for guessing meanings from context.

sentences.kr: 요즘은 예전보다 자막 없이도 더 많이 이해할 수 있게 되었어요.
sentences.en: These days, I can understand much more even without subtitles.

quiz.q:
저는 무엇을 보면서 한국어를 공부해요?
(What do I watch while studying Korean?)

처음에는 무엇이 없으면 이해하기 어려웠어요?
(What made understanding difficult at first?)

같은 장면을 여러 번 보면서 무엇을 익히게 되었어요?
(What did I become familiar with?)

모르는 단어가 나오면 어떻게 해요?
(What do I do when I see unknown words?)

요즘은 무엇 없이도 더 많이 이해할 수 있어요?
(What can I understand more without now?)

sentences.kr: 외국어를 배우면서 친구를 만드는 것은 매우 중요해요.
sentences.en: Making friends while learning a language is very important.

sentences.kr: 교과서에서는 배울 수 없는 표현을 들을 수 있기 때문이에요.
sentences.en: Because you can hear expressions that cannot be learned from textbooks.

sentences.kr: 처음에는 실수할까 봐 말하기가 조금 부담스러웠어요.
sentences.en: At first, I was nervous about speaking because I was afraid of making mistakes.

sentences.kr: 하지만 친구들은 제 실수를 이해해 주고 자연스럽게 고쳐 줬어요.
sentences.en: But my friends understood my mistakes and corrected them naturally.
Thanks to that, I was able to gain confidence.

sentences.kr: 지금은 새로운 사람을 만나는 것이 훨씬 편해졌어요.
sentences.en: Now meeting new people has become much easier.

quiz.q:
무엇을 배우면서 친구를 만드는 것이 중요해요?
(While learning what is making friends important?)

교과서에서는 배울 수 없는 것은 무엇이에요?
(What can't be learned from textbooks?)

처음에는 왜 말하기가 부담스러웠어요?
(Why was speaking difficult at first?)

친구들은 실수를 어떻게 했어요?
(What did friends do about mistakes?)

덕분에 무엇을 얻었어요?
(What did I gain?)

sentences.en: These days many people study at cafes.

sentences.kr: 집보다 집중이 더 잘된다고 생각하는 사람도 있어요.
sentences.en: Some people think they can concentrate better than at home.

sentences.kr: 적당한 소음이 오히려 공부에 도움이 된다고 느끼기 때문이에요.
sentences.en: Because they feel a moderate amount of noise actually helps them study.

sentences.kr: 저도 중요한 시험이 있을 때는 카페를 자주 이용해요.
sentences.en: I also often use cafes when I have an important exam.

sentences.kr: 하지만 사람이 너무 많으면 집중하기 어려울 때도 있어요.
sentences.en: However, it can be difficult to concentrate when there are too many people.

sentences.kr: 그래서 저는 비교적 조용한 카페를 찾는 편이에요.
sentences.en: So I tend to look for relatively quiet cafes.

quiz.q:
요즘은 어디에서 공부하는 사람이 많아요?
(Where do many people study these days?)

어떤 사람들은 카페에서 무엇이 더 잘된다고 생각해요?
(What do some people think is better at cafes?)

무엇이 공부에 도움이 된다고 느껴요?
(What is felt to help studying?)

저는 언제 카페를 자주 이용해요?
(When do I often use cafes?)

저는 어떤 카페를 찾는 편이에요?
(What kind of cafe do I tend to look for?)

enTitle: The Pros and Cons of Online Shopping

sentences.kr: 온라인 쇼핑은 이제 많은 사람들의 일상이 되었어요.
sentences.en: Online shopping has become part of everyday life for many people.

sentences.kr: 집에서 편하게 물건을 주문할 수 있다는 점은 큰 장점이에요.
sentences.en: Being able to order products comfortably from home is a major advantage.

sentences.kr: 반면에 직접 상품을 확인할 수 없다는 단점도 있어요.
sentences.en: On the other hand, there is also the disadvantage of not being able to inspect products directly.

sentences.kr: 실제로 사진과 실제 제품이 다른 경우도 종종 있다고 해요.
sentences.en: In fact, people say that the actual product is sometimes different from the photos.

sentences.kr: 그럼에도 불구하고 편리함 때문에 온라인 쇼핑을 이용하는 사람은 계속 늘어나고 있어요.
sentences.en: Nevertheless, more and more people continue to use online shopping because of its convenience.

sentences.kr: 소비자들은 상품 정보를 꼼꼼하게 확인할 필요가 있어요.
sentences.en: Consumers need to carefully check product information.

quiz.q:
온라인 쇼핑의 큰 장점은 무엇이에요?
(What is a major advantage of online shopping?)

온라인 쇼핑의 단점은 무엇이에요?
(What is a disadvantage of online shopping?)

사진과 실제 제품이 어떻게 될 때가 있어요?
(What can happen with photos and actual products?)

사람들은 왜 계속 온라인 쇼핑을 이용해요?
(Why do people continue using online shopping?)

소비자들은 무엇을 확인할 필요가 있어요?
(What should consumers check?)

sentences.kr: 외국어를 배우는 과정에서 실수는 피할 수 없는 일이에요.
sentences.en: Mistakes are unavoidable when learning a foreign language.

sentences.kr: 저도 한국어를 배우면서 여러 번 실수를 했어요.
sentences.en: I also made many mistakes while learning Korean.

sentences.kr: 한 번은 전혀 다른 뜻의 단어를 사용해서 친구들을 웃게 만든 적이 있어요.
sentences.en: Once, I used a word with a completely different meaning and made my friends laugh.
At that time, I felt very embarrassed.

sentences.kr: 하지만 그런 경험 덕분에 같은 실수를 반복하지 않게 되었어요.
sentences.en: However, thanks to that experience, I stopped repeating the same mistakes.

sentences.kr: 실수는 부끄러운 일이 아니라 성장 과정의 일부라고 생각해요.
sentences.en: I think mistakes are not something to be ashamed of but part of the learning process.

quiz.q:
외국어를 배우는 과정에서 실수는 어때요?
(What are mistakes like in language learning?)

친구들을 웃게 만든 이유는 무엇이에요?
(Why did the friends laugh?)

그 당시에는 어떤 기분이었어요?
(How did I feel at that time?)

그 경험 덕분에 무엇을 하지 않게 되었어요?
(What did I stop doing?)

실수는 무엇의 일부라고 생각해요?
(What are mistakes part of?)

sentences.kr: 해외에서 유학하는 것은 기대되는 일이기도 하지만 쉽지 않은 일이기도 해요.
sentences.en: Studying abroad is exciting, but it is not always easy.

sentences.kr: 새로운 환경에 적응해야 하고 모든 일을 스스로 해결해야 하기 때문이에요.
sentences.en: Because you have to adapt to a new environment and solve everything yourself.

sentences.kr: 특히 처음에는 언어 때문에 어려움을 겪는 경우가 많아요.
sentences.en: Especially at first, many people struggle because of the language.

sentences.kr: 하지만 시간이 지나면서 점차 익숙해지게 돼요.
sentences.en: However, as time passes, you gradually become accustomed to it.

sentences.kr: 또한 다양한 나라의 사람들을 만나면서 시야를 넓힐 수 있어요.
sentences.en: You can also broaden your perspective by meeting people from different countries.

sentences.kr: 유학 생활은 힘들기도 하지만 많은 것을 배울 수 있는 소중한 경험이에요.
sentences.en: Studying abroad can be difficult, but it is a valuable experience from which you can learn a lot.

quiz.q:
유학 생활은 어떤 일이에요?
(What kind of experience is studying abroad?)

왜 쉽지 않은 일이에요?
(Why is it not easy?)

처음에는 무엇 때문에 어려움을 겪어요?
(What causes difficulties at first?)

시간이 지나면서 어떻게 돼요?
(What happens as time passes?)

유학 생활은 어떤 경험이에요?
(What kind of experience is studying abroad?)

sentences.en: I take a walk in the morning.
I go to a park near my house.
The air is cool, so I feel good.
There are also many people who bring dogs.
After taking a walk, I return home.
And then I eat breakfast.

quiz.q:
저는 언제 산책을 해요?
(When do I take a walk?)

저는 어디에 가요?
(Where do I go?)

공기가 어때요?
(How is the air?)

사람들은 무엇을 데리고 와요?
(What do people bring?)

산책 후에 무엇을 먹어요?
(What do I eat after the walk?)

sentences.en: I like Korean food.
I especially eat kimchi stew often.
Kimchi stew is a little spicy.
But it is really delicious.
My friend likes kimchi stew too.
So we go to a restaurant together.
There are many customers at the restaurant.

quiz.q:
저는 무엇을 좋아해요?
(What do I like?)

저는 무엇을 자주 먹어요?
(What do I eat often?)

김치찌개는 어때요?
(How is kimchi stew?)

저는 누구와 식당에 가요?
(Who do I go to the restaurant with?)

식당에는 누가 많아요?
(Who are there many of at the restaurant?)

sentences.en: I use the subway often.
The subway is fast and convenient.
There are many people during rush hour.
So sometimes it is hard to sit down.
But it is more punctual than the bus.
I listen to music on the subway.

quiz.q:
저는 무엇을 자주 이용해요?
(What do I use often?)

지하철은 어때요?
(How is the subway?)

언제 사람이 많아요?
(When are there many people?)

출근 시간에는 무엇이 어려울 때도 있어요?
(What can be hard during rush hour?)

저는 지하철에서 무엇을 들어요?
(What do I listen to on the subway?)

enTitle: Preparing for a Trip to Korea

sentences.kr: 저는 다음 달에 한국으로 여행을 갈 예정이에요.
sentences.en: I am planning to travel to Korea next month.

sentences.kr: 그래서 비행기 표와 숙소를 미리 예약했어요.
sentences.en: So I booked my flight ticket and accommodation in advance.

sentences.kr: 여행 계획을 세우면서 가 보고 싶은 장소를 찾아보고 있어요.
sentences.en: While making my travel plan, I am looking up places I want to visit.

sentences.kr: 특히 경복궁과 한강공원에 꼭 가 보고 싶어요.
sentences.en: I especially want to visit Gyeongbokgung Palace and Hangang Park.

sentences.kr: 여행 전에 기본적인 표현도 다시 복습하려고 해요.
sentences.en: Before the trip, I plan to review basic expressions again.
The more I prepare, the more I look forward to the trip.

quiz.q:
저는 언제 한국으로 여행을 갈 예정이에요?
(When am I planning to travel to Korea?)

무엇을 미리 예약했어요?
(What did I book in advance?)

가 보고 싶은 장소를 어떻게 하고 있어요?
(What am I doing with places I want to visit?)

어디에 꼭 가 보고 싶어요?
(Where do I really want to go?)

여행 전에 무엇을 복습하려고 해요?
(What do I plan to review before the trip?)

enTitle: The Pros and Cons of Translation Apps

sentences.en: These days, many people use translation apps.

sentences.kr: 번역기는 모르는 단어를 빠르게 이해하는 데 도움이 돼요.
sentences.en: Translation apps help people quickly understand unknown words.

sentences.kr: 반면에 문맥에 따라 의미가 달라지는 표현은 정확하게 번역하지 못할 때도 있어요.
sentences.en: On the other hand, expressions whose meanings change depending on context are sometimes not translated accurately.

sentences.kr: 그래서 번역기에만 의존하는 것은 좋은 방법이 아니라고 생각해요.
sentences.en: So I think relying only on translation apps is not a good method.

sentences.kr: 외국어 실력을 향상시키기 위해서는 직접 이해하려는 노력도 필요해요.
sentences.en: To improve foreign language skills, effort to understand directly is also necessary.

quiz.q:
요즘 많은 사람들이 무엇을 사용해요?
(What do many people use these days?)

번역기는 무엇에 도움이 돼요?
(What do translation apps help with?)

무엇에 따라 의미가 달라질 수 있어요?
(What can meanings change depending on?)

번역기에만 의존하는 것은 어때요?
(How is relying only on translation apps?)

외국어 실력을 향상시키기 위해 무엇이 필요해요?
(What is needed to improve language skills?)

sentences.kr: 다른 문화를 이해하는 것은 단순히 언어를 배우는 것만으로는 충분하지 않아요.
sentences.en: Understanding another culture is not achieved simply by learning the language.

sentences.kr: 같은 표현이라도 문화적 배경에 따라 다르게 해석될 수 있기 때문이에요.
sentences.en: This is because even the same expression can be interpreted differently depending on cultural background.

sentences.kr: 따라서 외국어를 공부할 때는 역사와 생활 방식에도 관심을 가질 필요가 있어요.
sentences.en: Therefore, when studying a foreign language, it is necessary to pay attention to history and ways of life as well.

sentences.kr: 문화에 대한 이해가 깊어질수록 사람들과 더욱 자연스럽게 소통할 수 있어요.
sentences.en: The deeper your understanding of culture becomes, the more naturally you can communicate with people.

sentences.kr: 결국 언어 학습은 문화 학습과 함께 이루어져야 한다고 할 수 있어요.
sentences.en: In the end, language learning should take place together with cultural learning.

quiz.q:
문화를 이해하는 것은 무엇만으로 충분하지 않아요?
(What alone is not enough to understand culture?)

같은 표현도 무엇에 따라 다르게 해석될 수 있어요?
(What can the same expression be interpreted differently depending on?)

외국어를 공부할 때 무엇에도 관심을 가질 필요가 있어요?
(What should we also pay attention to when studying a foreign language?)

문화 이해가 깊어질수록 어떻게 소통할 수 있어요?
(As cultural understanding deepens, how can we communicate?)

언어 학습은 무엇과 함께 이루어져야 해요?
(What should language learning happen together with?)
